_**Agent (default)**_


Here’s what was added and how to use it.

### File
**`public/asaastance-logo.svg`** — transparent background, all strokes **`#FFFFFF`**, rounded caps/joins, with `<title>` + `aria-labelledby` for accessibility.

### Usage (Next.js)
Reference it like any static asset:

```tsx
import Image from "next/image";

<Image src="/asaastance-logo.svg" alt="Asaastance" width={48} height={48} priority />
```

Or inline the SVG if you need `currentColor` later (you’d change `stroke="#FFFFFF"` to `stroke="currentColor"` and set `text-white` on the parent).

### Note on fidelity
There is no image tracer (e.g. Potrace) in this environment, so the paths are a **manual vector interpretation** of your “A” (left ribbon, serif top, double right leg, crossbar + stepped spiral, split base). It should read clearly as the same mark, but it may not match the PNG **pixel-perfectly**. If you need an exact match, open the PNG in Figma / Illustrator, trace or refine paths, and replace this file—or run a proper trace locally and swap the SVG.

If you want, we can next wire this into your header or favicon and tune stroke widths for your real background color.

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Explaining logo format**

I'm explaining that the SVG is actually a base64-encoded PNG and won't scale or recolor easily. I'm weighing options for the logo: true vector trace, hand-crafted SVG, or CSS filter workaround for the base64-encoded PNG. </details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_


That file is **not a real vector logo** in practice. Here’s what it actually is:

### What’s inside
- The root is an SVG, but the artwork is a **full‑size PNG embedded as base64** and painted with a `<pattern>` on a `<rect>` (`fill="url(#pattern0_5_4)"`).
- So you still have a **raster image** (here 1024×1024 in the data URL), not paths you can cleanly recolor as vector.

### Why that’s a problem for your goals
1. **File size** — On the order of **hundreds of KB** for one “SVG,” which is poor for a nav logo / favicon.
2. **Not true SVG** — Zooming or large print won’t stay sharp like path-based art.
3. **Plain white** — The **gradient is baked into the PNG**. Turning it into flat white means **image processing** (e.g. threshold + mask, or editing in Figma/Illustrator), not a simple `fill="#fff"` on paths.

### What to use instead
- **`public/asaastance-logo.svg`** — Real strokes, white, small, suitable as the official mark **unless** you need a pixel-perfect match to the raster.
- **Better export** — Re-export from the design tool as **actual vectors** (paths only, no embedded image), or run a **proper trace** (Illustrator Image Trace, Vector Magic, etc.) and then set fill to white.

So: **as an “SVG,” this one is mainly a wrapper around a PNG** — I wouldn’t adopt it as the main official vector logo. If you want, we can add a **small optimized PNG/WebP** for places that need the exact gradient look, and keep the hand-built SVG for white-on-dark branding.

_**Agent (default)**_


Here’s what **Group 3.svg** is and what we did with it.

### What this file really is
It’s **still not a path-based vector**. Structure:

1. **Alpha mask** built from the same **embedded 1024×1024 PNG** (huge base64 blob).
2. A **single rectangle** filled with a solid color, clipped by that mask.

So the silhouette is **raster** (mask from the bitmap); only the fill is “vector” (one `<rect>`). That’s why the file is **~670 KB**—same class of export as your other “SVG.”

### What we changed for you
- Copied it to **`public/asaastance-logo-figma.svg`** in your repo.
- Replaced **`#0766EE` → `#FFFFFF`** so it reads as a **flat white** mark on transparent (good for dark headers).
- Set **`role="img"`**, **`<title id="asaastance-logo-title">`**, and **`aria-labelledby`** for accessibility.

Use it like:

```tsx
<Image src="/asaastance-logo-figma.svg" alt="Asaastance" width={130} height={107} />
```

(`viewBox` is `519×428`; keep aspect ratio.)

### Practical recommendation
| Asset | Best for |
|--------|-----------|
| **`asaastance-logo.svg`** | Small file, real strokes, crisp at any size (approximate geometry). |
| **`asaastance-logo-figma.svg`** | **Pixel-accurate** shape vs your design, but **large** and still **mask + bitmap**. |

For production, ideal follow-up is a Figma export of **real outlines** (no “Copy as PNG” / no rasterized effects), or one **optimized PNG/WebP** for this exact artwork plus the lightweight SVG where you don’t need a perfect match.

If you want this to be the default everywhere, say so and we can point the header/favicon at `asaastance-logo-figma.svg` and optionally add a smaller external PNG to shrink payload.

---

_**User**_

This is still the logo 

---

_**Agent (default)**_


Here’s what was wrong and what we did.

### What was wrong
`public/asaastance-logo.svg` was still the **abstract “A”** from the earlier image. Your **real** brand is the **rounded badge + stack icon + “Asaastance”** wordmark—the same idea as in your footer (`Footer.tsx`: `rounded-lg` tile + layered paths + `font-display` name).

### What’s in place now
**`public/asaastance-logo.svg`** is updated to that **horizontal lockup**, all in **`#FFFFFF`** on a transparent background:

1. **Mark** – Same **layers** geometry as the footer (`M12 2L2 7…` plus the two chevrons), inside a **`rx="12"`** square (aligned with `rounded-lg` at this size).
2. **White badge with cutouts** – The stack is implemented with a **mask** so the diamond and lines are **knockouts** through the white tile (like white ink on a dark header: the stack shows as the **background** color). That matches “blue tile + white stack,” inverted for a **white** logo.
3. **Wordmark** – **`Asaastance`** as `<text>` with **`Montserrat`** first (same as your app’s display font when the SVG is **inlined**). In a plain **`<img>`**, the OS may substitute **system-ui** unless you inline the SVG or embed the font.

### Files unchanged
- **`asaastance-logo-figma.svg`** is still the huge raster-in-SVG “A”; it doesn’t match this lockup. You can delete it later if you don’t need it.

### Using it
```tsx
<Image src="/asaastance-logo.svg" alt="Asaastance" width={268} height={56} className="h-8 w-auto" priority />
```
Use it on **dark or colored** bands so the knocked-out stack stays visible. If you need a variant for **white** pages (dark ink, no knockout), say so and we can add a second SVG (e.g. `asaastance-logo-dark.svg`).
